commits.body:
The renaming saga finds its close,
What changed, what stayed — the summary shows.
The ghost icon lingers, biding time,
But TermSurf now runs, and that's sublime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The icons came, the icons went,
Their purpose never quite was meant.
Icon Composer held the throne—
We'll leave that ghost face well alone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The icon fought back and won the round,
Icon Composer's format too tightly bound.
So we'll undo the swap,
Let the ghost icon stop,
Till a proper replacement is found.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The ghost still haunts the Dock, it seems—
Two culprits lurk behind the scenes:
Icon Composer holds the throne,
And debug builds have minds their own.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The ghost has shed its spectral guise,
Now TermSurf greets the user's eyes.
From menu bar to CLI help,
The surfer rides where phantoms yelped—
Though icons cling to ghostly ties.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Eight steps to shed the ghostly name,
From plist files to icon frame.
The CLI, the config, the About view too—
One experiment to make it through.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Update rename plan to modify macos/ directly
commits.body:
No need for a parallel directory—
We're forking, not preserving parity.
The ghost can change its name in place,
No second folder, no duplicate space.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A ghost once haunted every string,
Each menu, path, and CLI thing.
We mapped each trace from ts1's deed—
Now ts5 has its renaming screed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A name too long, a prefix too proud,
Now shorter and leaner, unbowed.
vendor/chromium's gone,
Just one carries on—
Plain "chromium/" stands in the crowd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Three changes planned, three changes done,
Three experiments till subtree won.
The repo's reshaped,
The docs have escaped—
Issue 418, you've had your run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Six steps now guide the upstream dance,
No more leaving merges up to chance.
Subtree pull, not merge—
Lest renames emerge—
And ts1? It stays in its trance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The skill shed its weight, grew lean and thin,
WezTerm and cef-rs no longer within.
Just Ghostty remains,
One doc that explains—
Let the upstream merging begin.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The skill once read from an old address,
Now ghostty.md handles the Ghostty mess.
Each upstream its own doc,
Like keys to a lock—
The right guide for each merge, no less.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two forks diverged in a repo wide,
One frozen still, one active with pride.
ts1 rests in peace,
ts5 gets the lease—
And subtree pull shall be our guide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Five generations now stand in line,
Each folder a chapter, each version a sign.
The gitignore grew, the skills learned new tricks,
CLAUDE.md got the memo: ts5's in the mix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The tree is planted, roots run deep,
Now docs and configs need their sweep.
Seven steps to tend the ground—
Gitignore, skills, and CLAUDE.md bound.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Third time's the charm, they say—
Subtree add saved the day.
ts5/ stands tall and neat,
Ghostty's code, fully complete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
git-subtree-dir: ts5
git-subtree-mainline: cf2a83931d66db142509e52339d1d8426bafdb93
git-subtree-split: 61e347a2c278263dd1392c324feaee5fb9712ee5
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The merge strategy kept getting burned,
So a subtree lesson was learned.
No fork point, no fuss,
Just add prefix for us—
A simpler approach, well-earned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
We silenced renames, hoping for peace,
But deletions arose in their place.
No ts5 in sight,
Just root-level blight—
The subtree strategy falls from grace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Rename detection stole the show,
So we'll tell it not to go.
With no-renames in the mix,
Subtree mapping finally clicks?

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The subtree merge had a plan,
Eight steps from beginning to end.
But rename detection
Ignored our direction—
Now it's Experiment 1, my friend.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
No read-tree tricks, no ours deceit—
Just subtree merge, clean and neat.
Since Ghostty's history's already ours,
One flag maps files to ts5's bowers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 418: design Change 3 in nine steps
commits.body:
A ghost shall rise in ts5's domain,
Imported fresh, no WKWebView chain.
Read-tree plants roots, merge-ours connects,
Subtree strategy for future effects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The cef-rs crate wandered free,
At root level for all to see.
Now vendor/ keeps things neat and tidy,
With analysis repos tucked beside-y.
Thirteen paths in ts3 rewritten—
By this new structure, we are smitten.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 418: design Change 2 in seven steps
commits.body:
The vendors were scattered across the root floor,
Now they'll gather in vendor/ behind one front door.
cef-rs gets git-mv'd (it's tracked, you see),
While the rest simply walk there — gitignored and free.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The submodule broke free, declared "I'm standalone!"
Sixty-two gigs of git data, no longer in chain.
From ts4 it rose to the root of the tree,
Gitignored and independent — finally free.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
We dropped origin — there's no remote host,
Just upstream from Google, the canonical post.
The steps got reordered (two before three),
So git data moves out before we set it free.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 418: origin comes home to roost
commits.body:
GitHub said "too large, no thanks,"
So origin stays on local ranks.
Upstream now points to Google's source,
The canonical Chromium, straight from the horse.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 418: fix .git pointer and file list
commits.body:
A .git file with a relative trail
Would snap in two when we move the mail.
So first we swap it for the real thing,
And list the poet among files to bring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Each branch gets a row, each row a link,
So none are lost in chromium's drink.
The doc remembers every fork we've made,
A ledger kept so history won't fade.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The poet's quill once tracked a submodule's fate,
Now chromium's free—the verse must update.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 418: detail Change 1 in ten steps
commits.body:
Ten steps to free the chromium tree:
Push branches, kill the submodule's key,
Move the dir, fix every path,
Then document the aftermath.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A scaffold stands, awaiting brick,
Change one is first—let's make it quick.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A submodule of chromium's girth
Made git status slow as birth.
So we cut the cord, set it free—
Now status runs instantly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Five generations, short and sweet,
ts1 through ts5, the naming's neat.
Who needs long names when three will do?
The ghost moves in to room ts2… wait, five.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 418: Ghostty gets its own room
commits.body:
Not at the root, but one floor down,
The ghost resides in its own town.
No README clash, no docs collide—
Each fork stays neatly on its side.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Before we move, we push our art,
Seven branches to the chart.
And every path that points the way
Gets checked, lest content_shell go astray.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 418: chromium docs find their home
commits.body:
The branch and commit, once spread thin,
Now live in docs/chromium.md within.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The folders shift, the paths align,
Chromium freed from ts4's confine.
Ghostty shall reign at root's front door,
While vendors nest on a lower floor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The ghost prevailed, the wez withdrew,
One pane to change, not twenty-two.
With Metal bones and Zig for brains,
It renders browsers inside panes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two terminals weighed upon the scale,
One Zig, one Rust, each with a tale.
Stars and forks and panes compared,
No stone unturned, no detail spared.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two terminals enter, one terminal leaves—
A fork is forever, so nobody grieves
Without research first, from panes to the core,
To find which one's worth maintaining evermore.
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Three languages walked into a bar—
C++, Swift, and Rust from afar.
Each rendered two panes,
At sixty-frame gains,
And none was the bottleneck star.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two viewports walk into a render pass,
Each drawing a quad through its own looking glass.
Left pane, right pane, 120 combined—
The Rust receiver leaves no frame behind.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
One pane was fine, but the goal demands two—
Same shader, same pass, just a viewport askew.
Left half for A, right half for B,
Six small changes, and two panes we'll see.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A function pretended to be a variable,
The CPU jumped to data — quite terrible.
We fixed that, then found
The wrong flag passed around,
Now Rust renders IOSurfaces, all seven unknowns severable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A dash and a flag, so alike yet so wrong,
--service sang softly, but not the right song.
--xpc-service was waiting, the true name all along—
No code need be changed, just the words we pass on.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The Rust receiver built and listened,
Its XPC ears perked, pipeline glistened.
But mach_task_self_ was a variable, not a call—
The CPU jumped to data and took a great fall.
We fixed the crash, yet the sender stayed mute,
And silence, alas, is hard to refute.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles
